questions.title: Shortest Path in Campus Grid
questions.prompt: Given a grid with open cells, walls, a start, and a target, return the fewest moves needed using four-direction movement. Return -1 if unreachable.

examples.input: grid with S at (0,0), T at (2,2), walls blocking some cells

constraints: Use BFS for unweighted shortest path.
hints: BFS explores by distance layers.
Track visited cells to avoid loops.
The first time you reach the target is shortest.

questions.title: Course Plan Topological Order
questions.prompt: Given courses and prerequisite pairs, return a valid order to take all courses, or an empty list if a cycle exists.
constraints: A course may have multiple prerequisites.
hints: Build indegree counts.
Start with courses that have indegree zero.
If you cannot schedule every course, there is a cycle.

questions.title: Longest Increasing Subsequence Length
questions.prompt: Return the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence in a list of integers.
constraints: O(n log n) is ideal, O(n^2) is acceptable for discussion.
hints: A subsequence does not need adjacent items.
Track best tail values for each length.
Binary search where each number should replace a tail.

questions.title: Edit Distance
questions.prompt: Return the minimum number of insertions, deletions, and replacements needed to convert one string into another.
constraints: Strings may be empty.
hints: Use a 2D DP table.
Base cases convert from or to an empty string.
If characters match, reuse the diagonal value; otherwise add 1 to the best neighbor.

questions.title: LRU Cache Simulation
questions.prompt: Simulate an LRU cache with capacity k over get and put commands. Return outputs for get commands.

examples.input: capacity=2, put 1 A, put 2 B, get 1, put 3 C, get 2

constraints: A simple ordered structure is fine for V1.
hints: You need fast lookup and recency order.
Accessing a key makes it most recent.
When capacity is exceeded, evict the least recent key.

questions.title: Median of Two Sorted Lists
questions.prompt: Given two sorted lists, return the median value of the combined sorted values.
constraints: A merge-based solution is acceptable for V1.
hints: You only need values near the middle.
A simple merge can stop after reaching the median index.
Handle odd and even total lengths separately.

questions.title: Word Ladder Steps
questions.prompt: Given a start word, end word, and dictionary, return the minimum one-letter transformations needed to reach the end word.
constraints: Every intermediate word must be in the dictionary.
hints: This is an unweighted shortest path.
Generate neighbors that differ by one letter.
Use BFS from the start word.

questions.title: Expression Evaluator
questions.prompt: Evaluate a string expression containing non-negative integers, +, -, *, /, and spaces. Use integer division toward zero.
constraints: No parentheses in this version.
hints: Multiplication and division have higher precedence.
One stack can hold signed terms.
Apply + and - by pushing terms, * and / by modifying the top term.

questions.title: Trie Prefix Counts
questions.prompt: Build a structure that supports inserting words and returning how many inserted words start with a prefix.

examples.input: insert cat, car, dog; count ca

constraints: Words are lowercase letters.
hints: Each node stores children.
Also store a count of words passing through each node.
During prefix count, walk the prefix and return that node's count.

questions.title: Union Find Components
questions.prompt: Given n students and friendship pairs, return the number of connected friend groups.
constraints: Students are numbered 0 to n-1.
hints: Each student starts as their own parent.
Union the roots for each pair.
Count distinct roots at the end.

questions.title: Kth Largest Stream
questions.prompt: Process a stream of numbers and return the kth largest value after each insertion once at least k values exist.
constraints: Use a min-heap of size k conceptually.
hints: Keep only the k largest values seen so far.
The smallest of those k values is the kth largest overall.
When size exceeds k, remove the smallest.

questions.title: Decode Ways
questions.prompt: Given a string of digits, count how many ways it can be decoded where 1=A through 26=Z.
constraints: A leading zero cannot be decoded alone.
hints: Let dp[i] be ways to decode the first i characters.
A valid one-digit code adds dp[i-1].
A valid two-digit code from 10 to 26 adds dp[i-2].

questions.title: Minimum Meeting Rooms
questions.prompt: Given meeting intervals, return the minimum number of rooms needed so no meetings overlap in a room.
constraints: End equal to start can reuse a room.
hints: Sort starts and ends separately or use a heap.
A meeting starting before the earliest end needs a new room.
Otherwise reuse the room that ended.

questions.title: Clone Graph
questions.prompt: Given a graph node with neighbors, return a deep copy of the connected graph.
constraints: Node values are unique for this practice version.
hints: Use a map from original node to copied node.
DFS or BFS through neighbors.
Return existing copies when a node has already been cloned.

questions.title: Maximum Subarray With One Deletion
questions.prompt: Return the maximum subarray sum when you may delete at most one element.
constraints: Subarray must be non-empty.
hints: Track best sum ending here with no deletion.
Track best sum ending here with one deletion.
For one deletion, either delete current or extend a previous deleted state.

questions.title: Serialize Binary Tree
questions.prompt: Serialize a binary tree to a string and deserialize it back to the same shape.

examples.output: string that can rebuild the tree

constraints: Use null markers for missing children.
hints: Preorder traversal works well.
Write a marker for null children.
Deserialization consumes tokens in the same order.

questions.title: Alien Dictionary Order
questions.prompt: Given words sorted by an unknown alphabet, return one possible character order, or an empty string if invalid.
constraints: Words contain lowercase letters.
hints: Find the first differing character between adjacent words.
That difference creates a directed edge.
Topologically sort the character graph.

questions.title: Subarray Sum Equals K
questions.prompt: Return how many contiguous subarrays sum to k.
constraints: Numbers may be negative.
hints: Use running prefix sums.
If current_sum - k appeared before, those prefixes form valid subarrays.
Count how many times each prefix sum has appeared.

questions.title: Maximal Square
questions.prompt: Given a binary matrix, return the area of the largest square containing only 1s.
constraints: Matrix may be empty.
hints: DP value is the largest square ending at a cell.
For a 1 cell, use 1 plus the minimum of top, left, and top-left.
Track the largest side length.

questions.title: Rate Limiter
questions.prompt: Design a function that returns true if a user action is allowed under at most k actions per rolling time window.
constraints: Timestamps are non-decreasing per user.
hints: Store recent timestamps per user.
Remove timestamps outside the window.
Allow only if the remaining count is below k.
